content.text: I’ll inspect the trigger utility and daily interval trigger implementation to see how the next fire time is computed across DST.

content.content:

18	 */
19	
20	#endregion
21	
22	using Quartz.Util;
23	
24	namespace Quartz.Impl.Triggers;
25	
26	/// <summary>
27	/// A concrete implementation of DailyTimeIntervalTrigger that is used to fire a <see cref="IJobDetail"/>
28	/// based upon daily repeating time intervals.
29	/// </summary>
30	/// <remarks>
31	/// <para>
32	/// The trigger will fire every N (<see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger.RepeatInterval"/> ) seconds, minutes or hours
33	/// (see <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger.RepeatInterval"/>) during a given time window on specified days of the week.
34	/// </para>
35	/// <para>
36	/// For example#1, a trigger can be set to fire every 72 minutes between 8:00 and 11:00 everyday. It's fire times would
37	/// be 8:00, 9:12, 10:24, then next day would repeat: 8:00, 9:12, 10:24 again.
38	/// </para>
39	/// <para>
40	/// For example#2, a trigger can be set to fire every 23 minutes between 9:20 and 16:47 Monday through Friday.
41	/// </para>
42	/// <para>
43	/// On each day, the starting fire time is reset to startTimeOfDay value, and then it will add repeatInterval value to it until
44	/// the endTimeOfDay is reached. If you set daysOfWeek values, then fire time will only occur during those week days period. Again,
45	/// remember this trigger will reset fire time each day with startTimeOfDay, regardless of your interval or endTimeOfDay!
46	/// </para>
47	/// <para>
48	/// The default values for fields if not set are: startTimeOfDay defaults to 00:00:00, the endTimeOfDay default to 23:59:59,
49	/// and daysOfWeek is default to every day. The startTime default to current time-stamp now, while endTime has not value.
50	/// </para>
51	/// <para>
52	/// If startTime is before startTimeOfDay, then startTimeOfDay will be used and startTime has no affect other than to specify
53	/// the first day of firing. Else if startTime is  after startTimeOfDay, then the first fire time for that day will be the next
54	/// interval after the startTime. For example, if you set startingTimeOfDay=9am, endingTimeOfDay=11am, interval=15 mins, and startTime=9:33am,
55	/// then the next fire time will be 9:45pm. Note also that if you do not set startTime value, the trigger builder will default to current time, and current time
56	/// maybe before or after the startTimeOfDay! So be aware how you set your startTime.
57	/// </para>
58	/// <para>
59	/// This trigger also supports a "repeatCount" feature to limit the number of times the trigger
60	/// fires per day. Setting repeatCount=0 means the trigger will fire once per day at startTimeOfDay.
61	/// Setting repeatCount=N means the trigger will fire N+1 times per day (the initial fire plus N
62	/// repeats), then advance to the next valid day and repeat. Unlike SimpleTrigger which uses
63	/// repeatCount as a global total, DailyTimeIntervalTrigger applies repeatCount per day. The
64	/// default value of repeatCount is set to REPEAT_INDEFINITELY.
65	/// </para>
66	/// </remarks>
67	/// <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger"/>
68	/// <see cref="DailyTimeIntervalScheduleBuilder"/>
69	/// <since>2.0</since>
70	/// <author>James House</author>
71	/// <author>Zemian Deng saltnlight5@gmail.com</author>
72	/// <author>Nuno Maia (.NET)</author>
73	[Serializable]
74	#pragma warning disable CA1708
75	public sealed class DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl : AbstractTrigger, IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger
76	#pragma warning restore CA1708
77	{
78	    /// <summary>
79	    /// Used to indicate the 'repeat count' of the trigger is indefinite. Or in
80	    /// other words, the trigger should repeat continually until the trigger's
81	    /// ending timestamp.
82	    /// </summary>
83	    public const int RepeatIndefinitely = -1;
84	    private DateTimeOffset startTimeUtc;
85	    private DateTimeOffset? endTimeUtc;
86	    private DateTimeOffset? nextFireTimeUtc;
87	    private DateTimeOffset? previousFireTimeUtc;
88	    private int repeatInterval = 1;
89	    private IntervalUnit repeatIntervalUnit = IntervalUnit.Minute;
90	    private HashSet<DayOfWeek> daysOfWeek = null!;
91	    private TimeOfDay startTimeOfDay = null!;
92	    private TimeOfDay endTimeOfDay = null!;
93	    private bool complete;
94	    private int repeatCount = RepeatIndefinitely;
95	    internal TimeZoneInfo? timeZone;
96	
97	    // Serializing TimeZones is tricky in .NET Core. This helper will ensure that we get the same timezone on a given platform,
98	    // but there's not yet a good method of serializing/deserializing timezones cross-platform since Windows timezone IDs don't
99	    // match IANA tz IDs (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones). This feature is coming, but depending
100	    // on timelines, it may be worth doign the mapping here.
101	    // More info: https://github.com/dotnet/corefx/issues/7757
102	    private string? timeZoneInfoId
103	    {
104	        get => timeZone?.Id;
105	        set => timeZone = value is null ? null : TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(value);
106	    }
107	
108	    /// <summary>
109	    /// Create a  <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger"/> with no settings.
110	    /// </summary>
111	    /// <param name="timeProvider">Time provider instance to use, defaults to <see cref="TimeProvider.System"/></param>
112	    public DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl(TimeProvider? timeProvider = null) : base(timeProvider ?? TimeProvider.System)
113	    {
114	    }
115	
116	    /// <summary>
117	    /// Create a <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger" /> that will occur immediately, and
118	    /// repeat at the given interval.
119	    /// </summary>
120	    /// <param name="name"></param>
121	    /// <param name="startTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should begin occurring.</param>
122	    /// <param name="endTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should stop occurring.</param>
123	    /// <param name="intervalUnit">The repeat interval unit. The only intervals that are valid for this type of trigger are <see cref="IntervalUnit.Second"/>, <see cref="IntervalUnit.Minute"/>, and <see cref="IntervalUnit.Hour"/>.</param>
124	    /// <param name="repeatInterval"></param>
125	    /// <param name="timeProvider">Time provider instance to use, defaults to <see cref="TimeProvider.System"/></param>
126	    /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="name"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
127	    public DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl(
128	        string name,
129	        TimeOfDay startTimeOfDayUtc,
130	        TimeOfDay endTimeOfDayUtc,
131	        IntervalUnit intervalUnit,
132	        int repeatInterval,
133	        TimeProvider? timeProvider = null)
134	        : this(name, SchedulerConstants.DefaultGroup, startTimeOfDayUtc, endTimeOfDayUtc, intervalUnit, repeatInterval, timeProvider)
135	    {
136	    }
137	
138	    /// <summary>
139	    /// Create a <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger" /> that will occur immediately, and
140	    /// repeat at the given interval.
141	    /// </summary>
142	    /// <param name="name"></param>
143	    /// <param name="group"></param>
144	    /// <param name="startTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should begin occurring.</param>
145	    /// <param name="endTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should stop occurring.</param>
146	    /// <param name="intervalUnit">The repeat interval unit. The only intervals that are valid for this type of trigger are <see cref="IntervalUnit.Second"/>, <see cref="IntervalUnit.Minute"/>, and <see cref="IntervalUnit.Hour"/>.</param>
147	    /// <param name="repeatInterval"></param>
148	    /// <param name="timeProvider">Time provider instance to use, defaults to <see cref="TimeProvider.System"/></param>
149	    /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="name"/> or <paramref name="group"/> are <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
150	    public DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl(
151	        string name,
152	        string group,
153	        TimeOfDay startTimeOfDayUtc,
154	        TimeOfDay endTimeOfDayUtc,
155	        IntervalUnit intervalUnit,
156	        int repeatInterval,
157	        TimeProvider? timeProvider = null)
158	        : this(name, group, (timeProvider ?? TimeProvider.System).GetUtcNow(), null, startTimeOfDayUtc, endTimeOfDayUtc, intervalUnit, repeatInterval, timeProvider)
159	    {
160	    }
161	
162	    /// <summary>
163	    /// Create a <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger" /> that will occur at the given time,
164	    /// and repeat at the given interval until the given end time.
165	    /// </summary>
166	    /// <param name="name"></param>
167	    /// <param name="startTimeUtc">A <see cref="DateTimeOffset" /> set to the time for the <see cref="ITrigger" />to fire.</param>
168	    /// <param name="endTimeUtc">A <see cref="DateTimeOffset" /> set to the time for the <see cref="ITrigger" />to quit repeat firing.</param>
169	    /// <param name="startTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should begin occurring.</param>
170	    /// <param name="endTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should stop occurring.</param>
171	    /// <param name="intervalUnit">The repeat interval unit. The only intervals that are valid for this type of trigger are <see cref="IntervalUnit.Second"/>, <see cref="IntervalUnit.Minute"/>, and <see cref="IntervalUnit.Hour"/>.</param>
172	    /// <param name="repeatInterval">The number of milliseconds to pause between the repeat firing.</param>
173	    /// <param name="timeProvider">Time provider instance to use, defaults to <see cref="TimeProvider.System"/></param>
174	    /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="name"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
175	    public DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl(
176	        string name,
177	        DateTimeOffset startTimeUtc,
178	        DateTimeOffset? endTimeUtc,
179	        TimeOfDay startTimeOfDayUtc,
180	        TimeOfDay endTimeOfDayUtc,
181	        IntervalUnit intervalUnit,
182	        int repeatInterval,
183	        TimeProvider? timeProvider = null)
184	        : this(name, SchedulerConstants.DefaultGroup, startTimeUtc, endTimeUtc, startTimeOfDayUtc, endTimeOfDayUtc, intervalUnit, repeatInterval, timeProvider)
185	    {
186	    }
187	
188	    /// <summary>
189	    /// Create a <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger" /> that will occur at the given time,
190	    /// and repeat at the given interval until the given end time.
191	    /// </summary>
192	    /// <param name="name"></param>
193	    /// <param name="group"></param>
194	    /// <param name="startTimeUtc">A <see cref="DateTimeOffset" /> set to the time for the <see cref="ITrigger" />to fire.</param>
195	    /// <param name="endTimeUtc">A <see cref="DateTimeOffset" /> set to the time for the <see cref="ITrigger" />to quit repeat firing.</param>
196	    /// <param name="startTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should begin occurring.</param>
197	    /// <param name="endTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should stop occurring.</param>
198	    /// <param name="intervalUnit">The repeat interval unit. The only intervals that are valid for this type of trigger are <see cref="IntervalUnit.Second"/>, <see cref="IntervalUnit.Minute"/>, and <see cref="IntervalUnit.Hour"/>.</param>
199	    /// <param name="repeatInterval">The number of milliseconds to pause between the repeat firing.</param>
200	    /// <param name="timeProvider">Time provider instance to use, defaults to <see cref="TimeProvider.System"/></param>
201	    /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="name"/> or <paramref name="group"/> are <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
202	    public DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl(
203	        string name,
204	        string group,
205	        DateTimeOffset startTimeUtc,
206	        DateTimeOffset? endTimeUtc,
207	        TimeOfDay startTimeOfDayUtc,
208	        TimeOfDay endTimeOfDayUtc,
209	        IntervalUnit intervalUnit,
210	        int repeatInterval,
211	        TimeProvider? timeProvider = null)
212	        : base(name, group, timeProvider ?? TimeProvider.System)
213	    {
214	        StartTimeUtc = startTimeUtc;
215	        EndTimeUtc = endTimeUtc;
216	        RepeatIntervalUnit = intervalUnit;
217	        RepeatInterval = repeatInterval;
218	        StartTimeOfDay = startTimeOfDayUtc;
219	        EndTimeOfDay = endTimeOfDayUtc;
220	    }
221	
222	    /// <summary>
223	    /// Create a <see cref="IDailyTimeIntervalTrigger" /> that will occur at the given time,
224	    /// fire the identified job and repeat at the given
225	    /// interval until the given end time.
226	    /// </summary>
227	    /// <param name="name"></param>
228	    /// <param name="group"></param>
229	    /// <param name="jobName"></param>
230	    /// <param name="jobGroup"></param>
231	    /// <param name="startTimeUtc">A <see cref="DateTimeOffset" /> set to the time for the <see cref="ITrigger" />to fire.</param>
232	    /// <param name="endTimeUtc">A <see cref="DateTimeOffset" /> set to the time for the <see cref="ITrigger" />to quit repeat firing.</param>
233	    /// <param name="startTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should begin occurring.</param>
234	    /// <param name="endTimeOfDayUtc">The <see cref="TimeOfDay" /> that the repeating should stop occurring.</param>
235	    /// <param name="intervalUnit">The repeat interval unit. The only intervals that are valid for this type of trigger are <see cref="IntervalUnit.Second"/>, <see cref="IntervalUnit.Minute"/>, and <see cref="IntervalUnit.Hour"/>.</param>
236	    /// <param name="repeatInterval">The number of milliseconds to pause between the repeat firing.</param>
237	    /// <param name="timeProvider">Time provider instance to use, defaults to <see cref="TimeProvider.System"/></param>
238	    /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="name"/>, <paramref name="group"/>, <paramref name="jobName"/> or <paramref name="jobGroup"/> are <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
239	    public DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl(
240	        string name,
241	        string group,
242	        string jobName,
243	        string jobGroup,
244	        DateTimeOffset startTimeUtc,
245	        DateTimeOffset? endTimeUtc,
246	        TimeOfDay startTimeOfDayUtc,
247	        TimeOfDay endTimeOfDayUtc,
248	        IntervalUnit intervalUnit,
249	        int repeatInterval,
250	        TimeProvider? timeProvider = null)
251	        : base(name, group, jobName, jobGroup, timeProvider ?? TimeProvider.System)
252	    {
253	        StartTimeUtc = startTimeUtc;
254	        EndTimeUtc = endTimeUtc;
255	        RepeatIntervalUnit = intervalUnit;
256	        RepeatInterval = repeatInterval;
257	        StartTimeOfDay = startTimeOfDayUtc;
258	        EndTimeOfDay = endTimeOfDayUtc;
259	    }

content.text: I found the relevant logic in `DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.GetFireTimeAfter`; next I’ll add a temporary regression test that reproduces the extra fire time around the DST fall-back transition.

content.content: 1 #region License 2 3 /* 4 * All content copyright Marko Lahma, unless otherwise indicated. All rights reserved. 5 * 6 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may not 7 * use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain a copy 8 * of the License at 9 * 10 * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 11 * 12 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software 13 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT 14 * WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the 15 * License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations 16 * under the License. 17 * 18 */ 19 20 #endregion 21 22 using Quartz.Impl.Triggers; 23 using Quartz.Spi; 24 using Quartz.Util; 25 26 namespace Quartz; 27 28 /// 29 /// A implementation that build schedule for DailyTimeIntervalTrigger. 30 /// 31 /// 32 /// 33 /// This builder provide an extra convenient method for you to set the trigger's EndTimeOfDay. You may 34 /// use either endingDailyAt() or EndingDailyAfterCount() to set the value. The later will auto calculate 35 /// your EndTimeOfDay by using the interval, IntervalUnit and StartTimeOfDay to perform the calculation. 36 /// 37 /// 38 /// When using EndingDailyAfterCount(), you should note that it is used to calculating EndTimeOfDay. So 39 /// if your startTime on the first day is already pass by a time that would not add up to the count you 40 /// expected, until the next day comes. Remember that DailyTimeIntervalTrigger will use StartTimeOfDay 41 /// and endTimeOfDay as fresh per each day! 42 /// 43 /// 44 /// Quartz provides a builder-style API for constructing scheduling-related 45 /// entities via a Domain-Specific Language (DSL). The DSL can best be 46 /// utilized through the usage of static imports of the methods on the classes 47 /// , , 48 /// , , 49 /// and the various implementations. 50 /// 51 /// Client code can then use the DSL to write code such as this: 52 /// 53 /// IJobDetail job = JobBuilder.Create<MyJob>() 54 /// .WithIdentity("myJob") 55 /// .Build(); 56 /// 57 /// ITrigger trigger = TriggerBuilder.Create() 58 /// .WithIdentity(triggerKey("myTrigger", "myTriggerGroup")) 59 /// .WithDailyTimeIntervalSchedule(x => 60 /// x.WithIntervalInMinutes(15) 61 /// .StartingDailyAt(TimeOfDay.HourAndMinuteOfDay(8, 0))) 62 /// .Build(); 63 /// 64 /// scheduler.scheduleJob(job, trigger); 65 /// 66 /// 67 /// James House 68 /// Zemian Deng saltnlight5@gmail.com 69 /// Nuno Maia (.NET) 70 public sealed class DailyTimeIntervalScheduleBuilder : ScheduleBuilder 71 { 72 private readonly TimeProvider timeProvider; 73 74 private int interval = 1; 75 private IntervalUnit intervalUnit = IntervalUnit.Minute; 76 private HashSet? daysOfWeek; 77 private TimeOfDay? startTimeOfDayUtc; 78 private TimeOfDay? endTimeOfDayUtc; 79 private int repeatCount = DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.RepeatIndefinitely; 80 private TimeZoneInfo? timeZone; 81 82 private int misfireInstruction = MisfireInstruction.SmartPolicy; 83 84 /// 85 /// A set of all days of the week. 86 /// 87 /// 88 /// The set contains all values between and 89 /// 90 public static readonly IReadOnlyCollection AllDaysOfTheWeek; 91 92 /// 93 /// A set of the business days of the week (for locales similar to the USA). 94 /// 95 /// 96 /// The set contains all values between and 97 /// 98 public static readonly IReadOnlyCollection MondayThroughFriday; 99 100 /// 101 /// A set of the weekend days of the week (for locales similar to the USA). 102 /// 103 /// 104 /// The set contains and 105 /// 106 public static readonly IReadOnlyCollection SaturdayAndSunday; 107 108 static DailyTimeIntervalScheduleBuilder() 109 { 110 var allDaysOfTheWeek = new HashSet(); 111 var mondayThroughFriday = new HashSet(); 112 foreach (var dayOfWeek in Enum.GetValues()) 113 { 114 allDaysOfTheWeek.Add(dayOfWeek); 115 116 if (dayOfWeek >= DayOfWeek.Monday && dayOfWeek <= DayOfWeek.Friday) 117 { 118 mondayThroughFriday.Add(dayOfWeek); 119 } 120 } 121 122 SaturdayAndSunday = new HashSet 123 { 124 DayOfWeek.Sunday, 125 DayOfWeek.Saturday 126 }; 127 128 AllDaysOfTheWeek = allDaysOfTheWeek; 129 MondayThroughFriday = mondayThroughFriday; 130 AllDaysOfTheWeek = new HashSet(AllDaysOfTheWeek); Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.content: M src/Quartz/Impl/Triggers/DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.cs

content.text:
Implemented the DST fix in `src/Quartz/Impl/Triggers/DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.cs`.

Summary:
- `DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.GetFireTimeAfter` was calculating interval jumps using UTC/absolute elapsed time.
- Across DST fallback, a 24-hour interval could land at `23:01:05` on the same local calendar day instead of the next day’s configured daily start time.
- Updated the interval calculation to use local wall-clock `DateTime` arithmetic and then re-apply the correct timezone offset for the computed local fire time.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the reported Central European DST fallback scenario.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
- Confirmed it passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary regression test afterward, per instructions.

Current working tree:
- Only production source file modified:
  - `src/Quartz/Impl/Triggers/DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.cs`

No commits were created.

result:
Implemented the DST fix in `src/Quartz/Impl/Triggers/DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.cs`.

Summary:
- `DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.GetFireTimeAfter` was calculating interval jumps using UTC/absolute elapsed time.
- Across DST fallback, a 24-hour interval could land at `23:01:05` on the same local calendar day instead of the next day’s configured daily start time.
- Updated the interval calculation to use local wall-clock `DateTime` arithmetic and then re-apply the correct timezone offset for the computed local fire time.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the reported Central European DST fallback scenario.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
- Confirmed it passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary regression test afterward, per instructions.

Current working tree:
- Only production source file modified:
  - `src/Quartz/Impl/Triggers/DailyTimeIntervalTriggerImpl.cs`

No commits were created.
